risks: Consumption plan (Y1) is used instead of the recommended Flex Consumption (FC1); Y1 has cold starts, 10-minute max timeout, and no VNet integration support
Public network access is enabled on both Key Vault and Cosmos DB with no private endpoint or network isolation
Storage account local auth (shared key access) is not explicitly disabled, contrary to security best practices
Standard_LRS storage provides no geo-redundancy; data loss risk if the primary region fails
Single-region deployment (eastus) with no failover or zone redundancy for any resource
No explicit RBAC role assignments defined for the managed identity to access Storage, Cosmos DB, or Key Vault data planes
Missing WEBSITE_CONTENTAZUREFILECONNECTIONSTRING and WEBSITE_CONTENTSHARE app settings required by Consumption plan on Windows for dynamic scaling
No diagnostic settings (Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings) defined for the Function App
correctionsRecommended: Switch App Service Plan from Consumption (Y1) to Flex Consumption (FC1) per Azure Functions deployment best practices

Add RBAC role assignment resources for the managed identity: Storage Blob Data Owner, Storage Blob Data Contributor, Storage Queue Data Contributor, Storage Table Data Contributor, and Monitoring Metrics Publisher
Disable storage account local auth by setting allowSharedKeyAccess to false
Add WEBSITE_CONTENTAZUREFILECONNECTIONSTRING and WEBSITE_CONTENTSHARE app settings for Consumption plan compatibility, or migrate to FC1 which uses blob-based deployment
Add Microsoft.Insights/diagnosticSettings resource for the Function App to persist logs
Consider adding private endpoints for Key Vault and Cosmos DB to restrict public network access
Consider upgrading storage redundancy to ZRS or GRS for production resilience
Add resource tags for cost tracking and operational governance
